channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The VP debate was a return to the politics of yesteryear—except on Truth Social
item.description: Minutes before the start of the debate vice presidential debate, Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump made a commitment of nonpartisanship. “We’re going to keep it very straight, see how they do,” he said in a video posted to Truth Social, the social platform he owns.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: IBM opens first quantum computing center in Europe
item.description: IBM unveiled its first IBM Quantum Data Center outside the United States – and its second ever – in Ehningen, Germany.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Beware of fraudsters posing as government officials trying to steal your cash
item.description: CISA warns of scammers impersonating its officials. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges people to be alert and verify contacts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla Sees Global Deliveries Increase in Q3 Amid Low-Interest Financing and Price Cuts
item.description: Tesla's global deliveries see a boost in the third quarter, driven by low-interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts, and free charging. The company delivers 462,890 vehicles, surpassing analyst estimates, but shares drop due to market expectations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: iPhone's satellite messaging: A lifeline in Hurricane Helene's aftermath
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how iOS 18’s satellite messaging helps isolated residents seek help in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The role of IoT in shaping smart cities
item.description: As people increasingly migrate from rural areas to cities for better economic opportunities and improved living standards, modern municipalities are faced with challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: What lies beneath the Great Lakes' waves is largely unknown, but there's a new push to learn more about thousands of shipwrecks, underwater infrastructure and the impacts of climate change on the bottom of the world's largest freshwater system.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: It’s a wrap for the HoloLens 2 headset
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday confirmed it is no longer producing the HoloLens 2, the company’s second-generation augmented reality headset first launched five years ago at Mobile World Congress (MWC) in Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Verizon Nationwide Outage Affects Thousands, Leaving Customers in SOS Mode
item.description: Thousands of Verizon customers experience nationwide outage, stuck in SOS mode, learn how to troubleshoot and get out of it
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: This state is suing YouTube for fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: AppliedXL and Associated Press team up to use AI to sift through federal regulations
item.description: In an era when local newsrooms are stretched thinner than ever, keeping up with the constant deluge of government regulations and updates can feel impossible.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: US port strike has CIOs prepping for supply chain implications
item.description: Dockworkers have launched a widespread strike at ports across the eastern US from Texas to Maine, with wages and automation topping their list of labor concerns. The stoppage, the first in decades, could cause price hikes and supply shortages, depending on how long it lasts, the AP reports.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Previews: What’s in the latest build?
item.description: The Windows 11 2023 Update has been released, but behind the scenes, Microsoft is constantly working to improve the newest version of Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: California’s effort to find appropriate AI rules is a fool’s mission
item.description: When California Gov. Gavin Newsom vetoed a key piece of AI oversight legislation Sunday, he said he did so because the measure “falls short of providing a flexible, comprehensive solution to curbing the potential catastrophic risks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FAA confirms SpaceX Falcon 9 is grounded because of Crew-9 launch issue
item.description: The agency is investigating why the rocket's second stage missed its deorbit landing target zone after this weekend's Crew-9 launch.
item.dc:creator: Richard Tribou, The Mercury News

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: Australia’s government says a code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The battle cry of 2025: Do cloud local!
item.description: Microclouds can have many names and offer many features. They can be hosted by a regional cloud provider or specialize in a specific use case, such as the rise of GPU cloud providers focusing on the exploding AI space.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The worst programmer I know
item.description: If all code is legacy code, then we are all maintainers. I carry an image in my head of a vast sea of silent, diligent, dedicated coders who silently maintain much of the code that makes the world go round. I carry this image because I am one of those coders.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Germany’s antitrust authority to look into complaint against Microsoft
item.description: German file hosting cloud service provider Nextcloud appeared to have won a major victory Monday, when the country’s antitrust authority announced it would be initiating what the company described as “the first steps towards an investigation into anti-competitive behavior by Microsoft, including the
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Federal Trade Commission employees vote to unionize
item.description: FTC began the process to unionize back in 2023, raising concerns about their access to telework, potential politicization of the civil service and other issues. The post Federal Trade Commission employees vote to unionize first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Lionsgate's bold move into AI is about to change filmmaking forever
item.description: Lionsgate's revolutionary move to partner with tech company Runway to integrate AI into its production process is going to take the film industry by storm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How much are companies willing to spend to get workers back to the office?
item.description: With more and more companies wanting to bring employees back to the office, I pointed out last week the ill-kept secret that there’s a widespread aversion to open office floor plans — or activity-based workplaces, as they have often evolved into today — and that it partially explains why many employ
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Demystifying AI for the public sector
item.description: Government agencies are understandably hesitant about artificial intelligence. But with the right partners, AI can revolutionize government services. The post Demystifying AI for the public sector first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Police are probing apparent cyber vandalism on Wi-Fi networks at UK train stations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. transport officials and police said Thursday they are investigating a “cyber-security incident” after users of public Wi-Fi networks at the country’s biggest railway stations reported being shown anti-Muslim messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4
